auth_notes: Requires a Google Cloud Console project with the Google Calendar API enabled. Users must configure an OAuth consent screen and generate a Client ID and Client Secret. The redirect URI from n8n must be added to the authorized list in Google Cloud.
rate_limits: 1,000,000 requests per day per project; 10 queries per second (QPS) per user.
gotchas: Timezone mismatches: If the n8n environment and Google Calendar settings differ, event times may shift unexpectedly.
Token Expiration: OAuth refresh tokens expire every 7 days if the Google Cloud project is set to 'Testing' mode instead of 'Production'.
Recursive Loops: Workflows triggered by 'Event Updated' that also perform an 'Update' action on the same event can cause infinite execution loops.
client_must_provide: Specific Calendar ID (usually the primary email address)
research_notes: While the category is Email/Calendar, this analysis focuses on Google Calendar as the primary n8n node. Microsoft Outlook is the secondary alternative and requires Azure AD configuration which has higher auth complexity.

effort_recommendation.rationale: While the category is Email/Calendar, this analysis focuses on Google Calendar as the primary n8n node. Microsoft Outlook is the secondary alternative and requires Azure AD configuration which has higher auth complexity.
